cells.notes: Coverage: 15 records sampled via random offsets proportionally across 6 categories (User-Facing App 5, Infrastructure 3, Tooling 3, Protocol/Contract 2, Asset 1, Anchor 1) + 2 non-Live records (zenex Pre-Release, warpdrive Development) + 3 known entities (Freighter, Soroswap, StellarExpert). Bridges/ramps only appeared where randomly sampled (Tezoro, ZKLiquid); not focused on. STRENGTHS worth keeping: exact-name known-item is solid — 17/17 top-3 (15 rank-0), non-Live status does not hide records; curated rename aliases work (choppaddi→Fastbuka rank 0); parenthetical alias 'quasar' retrieves Lightsail Quasar at rank 1 behind Eiger's legitimately-distinct 'Quasar' oracle (NOT a dupe — different websites/products); gYEN retrieves fine despite null shortDescription ('gyen stablecoin' rank 0 via types). Not filed (within tolerance): 'lightsail network' ranks java-stellar-sdk #1 above the record literally named 'Lightsail Network (Quasar)' at #2 — top-3 satisfied but a mild authority-over-name-match smell consistent with the known ranking defect class; 'token launchpad' → aida #1 vs stellar-token-launchpad #2 is equally-valid ordering. Parent/product pair lightsail vs lightsail-network-quasar is a curation choice, not filed as dupe. Systemic pattern: the no-match semantic fallback is the single biggest risk in this cell — it converts every failed known-item lookup into a confident wrong answer (strict/'all keywords matched' label + 0.9 confidence + total==limit), which for agent consumers (Raven) is worse than an empty set. ~8 additional inspection calls (record compares, meta checks) supported the graded 45 probes.

Coverage: all 19 assigned types probed (Bridge skipped per instructions); ground truth = full 892-record category-browse dump (slug/types/status/prominence/description). Systemic patterns: (1) STRICT-AND GENERIC-NOUN TOKENS — appending 'apps'/'providers'/'platforms' to a type word excludes flagship records entirely (payment apps, custody providers; also streamingfast+space-and-time dropped from 'analytics platforms' though that top-10 stayed on-type, not filed); single-token and 'X on stellar'-without-noun controls are healthy. (2) TYPE-BLIND RANKING — multi-word phrasing lets off-type prose matches (prominence-weighted) beat on-type flagships (wallets/stablecoins/anchors); matches the known F1 structured-fields root. (3) STATUS DOWNRANKING WORKS — strong OK: Inactive slender (prom 80) and orbitcdp (70) appear nowhere in lending top-25 while all 20 Live Lending-typed records rank first; Development raum-network ranks below all Live prominents in dex browse. (4) Healthy browses: dex, lending, rpc (10/10 each), sdk, indexer, faucet, gaming, nft (litemint #1), analytics, ai (stellar-ai-agent-kit #1), social-impact (10/10), security-audits, payments (simple phrasing), remittances. (5) Hygiene: 59/892 Live records have empty shortDescription; two un-canonicalized dupe pairs found incidentally — a targeted dupe sweep (name/website normalization) would likely find more. okCount counts graded probes incl. controls; the two dupe-verification lookups and the corpus scan are not counted as probes.

Coverage caveats: bridges/ramps deliberately not probed (anti-anchoring) — supportedNetworks tested via non-bridge carriers (lobstr Wallet, ultra-stellar Security, xrpl queries); the field is 14/892 sparse and 11 of 14 carriers are Bridge-typed, so multi-chain attribute retrieval outside bridges is untestable. Systemic patterns: (1) structured-fields-drive-inclusion now demonstrably WORKS (null-prose audd retrieved for Australia via coverage.countries; anclap for Argentina without prose mention; scfAwarded param filters exactly and meta echoes it honestly) — the F1 class fix is visible in this cell; (2) the remaining attribute-lane blocker is the 26 ticker-stub Asset records where the coverage fields that would carry the attribute are empty AND prose is null — nothing for either keyword or structured inclusion to grab; (3) semantic fallback on no-hit queries serves sparse-corpus noise with always-high confidence (relevance:1) — a systemic trust-signal defect beyond this cell; (4) no country/demonym stemming (Argentina/Argentine/Argentinians). Sampling: all 892 browsable records fetched and grepped locally for ground truth; every coverage/seps carrier probed; scfAwarded graded on 3 type lanes (gaming 11/11, education 18/18, social impact 8/8 ground-truth recall).

Strong OK coverage on the cell's core: all 10 non-Live records (slender/keybase/orbitcdp/orbit-finance/zenex/helix/warpdrive/spectra-finance/ultra-swap/raum-network) serve honest status labels, and 9/10 resolve top-1 on known-item queries. Lifecycle ordering on the q-path is exemplary — q=lending/money market/flash loans/cdp all INCLUDE Inactive records but demote them to the bottom (sls-018 inclusion principle holds). The 7 human-verified defunct projects (eliodao/communidao/lumosdao/fxdao/instantdao/enerdao/thehub) are fully removed from the corpus, not mislabeled Live — their queries return only legitimate prose-mention hits (verified orion mentions FxDAO). confidence.freshness is status-wired (Live=1.0/Dev=0.7/Inactive=0.5) — a real discriminating signal contra the map agent's browse-only read, though ageDays is always null even when lastActivityAt exists. Dev/Pre-Release prose is consistent with status (zenex says 'currently on testnet'). lastActivityAt coverage 81/100 in Tooling sample. Systemic tension worth a design note: the Inactive demotion fights explicitly-NAMED status queries — 'is slender still active' and 'is keybase still active' rank the named record 5/5 below semantic noise (within tolerance, not filed); a named-record exemption from the demotion would fix the class. Caveat: temporal probes limited to search phrasing + leaderboard/changelog; no addedAt/launchedAt field exists anywhere, so 'newest additions to directory' is unanswerable by any lane.

Graded 20 consumer-intent probes (+6 aux ground-truth queries: q=philippines, q=yield, q=inflation, dup-pair lookups) against expectations derived from the records' own types/status/prominence/descriptions in the 892-record category browse. 13 probes fully OK: earn interest on savings (blend/ondo/glo-dollar/vaquita top-4), best stellar wallet + which wallet should i use (lobstr/xbull/freighter 1-3), crypto loan (8/10 lending records), send money to mexico (felix-pago #1) and kenya (boss-pay/changera — both descs mention Kenya), NFT art (litemint/rarible), accept crypto payments in store (7/10 genuine gateways incl. syklo WooCommerce), split bills ×2 (thin-but-honest, corpus genuinely lacks a bill-split app; sorosplits is revenue-sharing, not filed), pay at stores, invest in farming (microvault #3), music artists (tipper/skyhitz). SYSTEMIC PATTERNS: (1) phrasing fragility is the cell's dominant root — consumer filler words ('store my money', 'protect from inflation', 'for savings groups') act as extra AND-terms that shrink/warp the candidate set and exclude the records whose own data best matches the intent (findings 1, 5, 6; the known strict-AND bug class); simple phrasings of the same intents are near-perfect. (2) The semantic lane hard-caps at total=10 and its confidence scores routinely rank off-topic records (SDKs, anchors) ABOVE on-topic keyword hits (0.91-0.97 vs 0.6-0.75) — inverted trust signal for Raven (findings 2, 7, 10). (3) Hygiene: 4 unlinked duplicate pairs (canonicalSlug=null everywhere) and 2 empty-description Live rows surface on consumer queries. (4) Inactive records (slender, keybase) only ever appeared at list-bottoms with status visible — acceptable, not filed. Coverage caveat: no bridge/ramp/Etherfuse probes per anchoring rule; corridor coverage sampled PH/MX/KE only.

POSITIVES (half the probes OK): meta.superlativeNote is a genuinely good honesty mechanism and fired on 12/12 superlative phrasings tested (biggest/most used/best/largest/top/highest/leading/most popular/most active/fastest/top 5) with accurate text (order = relevance+prominence+liveness, null tvl = not-tracked-not-zero); tvlUSD/tvlAsOf served fresh (same-day DefiLlama); matchModeLabel honest; leaderboard rejects sort=tvl loudly (no silent-ignore EMPTY-DISHONEST found); Inactive slender ($93 tvl) never polluted a top; 'freighter or lobstr' + 'which is better lobstr or freighter' correctly serve both wallets top-2 (majority mode); 'biggest lending protocol' is exemplary (Blend #1 by its own $139M tvlUSD); 'best oracle' top-5 all oracles (Reflector #1); 'top amm' all-DEX top. SYSTEMIC ROOTS behind the 8 findings: (1) superlative adjectives are simultaneously detected (note fires) AND scored as keywords — records whose PROSE contains 'largest/most/leading' get relevance 1.0 → conf 0.92-0.97 'high', while true type-matched prominence-90 records sit at 0.60-0.65 'medium'; on this cell the confidence field ANTI-correlates with correctness, so the note's honesty is defeated by the scorer (an agent reads #1@0.97 as the superlative answer). (2) strict-AND over all tokens collapses recall pools (total=6-10 out of 888) on multi-token superlatives, dropping exactly the tvlUSD-bearing records; non-null tvlUSD drives neither inclusion nor ordering (same F1 structured-fields-not-inclusion root as the full-surface audit). (3) comparison 'X vs Y' phrasing defeats exact-name inclusion (sls-018/019 principle regression surface) and gets no note of any kind. (4) note-lexicon gap: 'safest' doesn't trigger it (results were fine there). (5) The note's 'Ecosystem aggregates → /api/analyze' pointer cannot answer TVL/usage/activity superlatives (validDimensions = all/hackathons/categories/funding only). COVERAGE CAVEATS: projects lane only per cell; 20 graded probes across dex/wallet/stablecoin/oracle/lending/nft/explorer/anchor/amm/payments/defi + 4 comparison phrasings; bridges/ramps not probed (allbridge/templar/etherfuse appeared only as ground-truth tvl rows, per no-anchoring rule); 'best block explorer' counted OK but note Rumble Fish (a dev agency that built an explorer) at #3 above Stellar Expert — borderline, top-1 was on-topic; 'largest anchor' counted OK though Yellow Card@0.92 over Moneygram prom-90@0.60 shows the same prose-'largest' inversion with an on-topic top-5.

Coverage: 41 probes over wallets/wallet-sdk, smart-wallet, passkey/webauthn, payments, indexer, nft, gaming/game, dao/governance, kyc, identity, audit, testing, mobile/ios/android/flutter, multisig, escrow + language filters (java/c/go/swift) + one /api/repos/explain routing check. Excluded bridges/amm/rwa/stablecoin/zk/oracles per brief (zk appears only incidentally via the gaming corpus, which is organically ZK-hackathon heavy). SYSTEMIC PATTERNS: (1) serve order partitions by codeVerified/stellarProof (verified block first) and within/across blocks is provably non-monotonic in the API's own `score` field — seen in nft, governance, payments, flutter, gaming; this is the single root behind 4 of 7 findings. To the cell question 'do topical repos with real codeVerified beat off-topic high-authority ones': codeVerified now BOOSTS strongly — sometimes over stronger textual matches (the inverse failure of the old authority-dominance class); raw repoScore authority no longer buries topicality in top ranks on these verticals (improvement vs the rwa→dfns era). (2) Case-variant duplicate indexing (owner and owner-lowercased as separate rows) with divergent repoScores — 4 pairs found without looking for them. (3) Owner-string substring matching lets org names hijack topical queries (hot-dao). POSITIVES worth keeping: q=wallet, q=indexer, q=audit (scout-audit rank 1), q=kyc, q=identity (did-stellar rank 1), q=testing, q=mobile (freighter-mobile rank 1), q=ios (soneso rank 1), q=escrow, q=multisig top-4, q=webauthn all grade OK top-5; explain?q=smart wallet routes to stellar/smart-wallet-demo-app with rich codeVerified. COVERAGE CAVEAT (not filed as a finding): kalepail/passkey-kit — the canonical Stellar passkey/smart-wallet reference implementation — is absent from the repo corpus entirely (q=passkey-kit total:0, q=kalepail returns only kale-sc + stellarauth-serverless); an agent asking for THE standard passkey reference gets SocketFi instead. Also note stellar/stellar-protocol has primaryLanguage 'RPC' — verified legit (GitHub linguist classifies .x XDR files as RPC), not a data bug.

Fair-grader summary: 41/45 OK — the identifier-form fix generalizes well beyond the original two test symbols. Verified across 17 distinct carrier repos (repoScore 20-77, i.e. long tail included) and ~11 topic areas: exact snake_case (7/7 at rank 1), exact CamelCase/camelCase incl. TS (8/8 top-5, mostly #1), cross-form variants (computeSwap→compute_swap carrier #2, liquidity_pool_trait→LiquidityPoolTrait carrier #1, space-separated forms all top-5), numeric-in-symbol (bn256_modulus #1), natural \"<concept> implementation\" phrasings (4/4 top-5), and low-score long-tail symbols (score-20 repos retrieved #1). Coverage caveats: (1) symbols only samplable from repos reachable via topic queries — the >200-offset tail could not be enumerated, so carriers there are untested; (2) many high-authority repos are scanned-with-empty-symbols (e.g. stellar/js-stellar-sdk codeDepth 0, symbols[]) — symbol-scan coverage itself is thin at the top of the corpus, which is a scan-coverage matter outside this cell but caps how useful the symbol lane can be. Systemic-but-not-filed: meta.counts.total is heavily sub-token-inflated on exact-symbol queries (VcVaultContract total=345, ApiDistributeEscrowEarnings total=140 — \"contract\"/\"escrow\" token matches); ranking still surfaces the exact carrier at #1 so agents are unharmed, but the totals overstate match counts. Explain lane on bare symbols behaves honestly (compute_swap → correct alternate + search_repos signpost).

Fair-grader summary: the canonical lane is GOOD where its keyword map fires — consensus, SCP, stellar consensus protocol, XDR, rpc server, soroban rpc, soroban sdk, ledger close, protocol upgrade, and transaction result codes (known-fixed, re-confirmed) all float the right SDF repos with meta.canonical, and canonical-routed explain answers are genuinely grounded in real code symbols (NominationProtocol/SCP classes, txnbuild+horizonclient, NewSimulateTransactionHandler, Transaction.sign/Keypair). 31/45 probes clean. Failures concentrate in three roots: (1) explain's non-canonical fallback is winner-take-all over authority-ranked search, so it either picks an off-topic high-scorer (scaffold-stellar, nethermind) or returns repo:null with a misleading alternate (phoenix for blend, moonlight for freighter, crossmint for soroban) — 6 of 13 explain probes failed this way; (2) the canonical index predates the stellar/go monorepo breakup — archived stellar/go still leads over in-corpus successors (stellar-horizon, go-stellar-sdk, galexie) and dead-ends explain with answered:false; (3) explicit repo names in q (anchor platform, js-stellar-base, stellar-cli) don't override keyword priority, producing answered:true responses whose text self-disclaims. Caveats: stellar/go stars=3/isArchived=true was verified TRUE against GitHub (do NOT 'fix' as data-wrong); passkey-kit is absent from the repos corpus entirely (project js-capacitor-passkey-kit has empty repos[]) — a coverage gap, not a retrieval bug, and it explains the crossmint routing on the passkey explain probe; explain's null answers are honestly labeled (note2), so no EMPTY-DISHONEST observed. Explain probes are slow (~10-60s) but deterministic across a retry (horizon null twice).

Corpus is impressively CURRENT on the big migration traps — this cell's headline risk mostly did NOT materialize: all build guides teach `stellar contract build` + wasm32v1-none (12 chunks checked, zero wasm32-unknown-unknown advice), setup page requires Rust 1.84+, every JS install command uses scoped @stellar/stellar-sdk / @stellar/freighter-api, and even querying the OLD target or old soroban-cli name serves current docs plus the rename meeting note. Clean OKs: deploy-to-testnet, faucet/friendbot, freighter-react, validator admin-guide, quickstart docker, SEP-24 anchor setup, passkeys, RPC providers, horizon payments, contract events, create-account, multisig, testnet-reset. Filter contract is honest: bogus source → 400 with validSources; source=sep honored. The staleness that DOES leak is structural, not editorial: (1) living version-history pages are chunked by era and freshness scoring is blind (publishedAt null on 100% of dev-docs) so 2022/2023 chunks surface on current-setup queries; (2) docusaurus author-archive pagination pages are indexed as duplicate docs. Both are ingest-side fixes (parse 'Last updated on' into publishedAt; canonicalize/skip /meetings/authors/* pagination URLs). Coverage caveat: dev-docs dominated ~93% of returned rows in this cell; sep/cap/audit appeared only on multisig and network queries — thin-source behavior not deeply probed here.

Coverage: SEP/CAP corpora are broad and healthy — SEPs 0001-0059 present incl. 7/11/14/16/34/44/45-48/50/52-56/59; CAPs 0001-0084 incl. all Soroban-era ones; no coverage gaps found in this cell. Concept-query content quality is strong: 21/29 graded probes had authoritative sep/cap/dev-docs in top-5 (SCP query even serves the actual SCP whitepaper PDF at #2). The failures are retrieval-mechanics, not corpus: (1) no lexical/exact-match lane means CAP-number lookup fails outright (SEPs saved only by dev-docs mirror-page titles); (2) best-chunk-per-doc dedupe is broken everywhere (worst under source= filters), directly contradicting the response's own scoreModel note; (3) meetings/* date pages, meetings/archive, and meetings/authors/* pages (incl. pagination pages titled just an author name) should likely be excluded or down-weighted at index time — they carry near-zero content and duplicate meeting one-liners under multiple URLs. Recurring generic catch-alls (How-To Guides, Stellar CLI Manual, list-of-operations) crowd top-3 on many queries but were within ordering-taste tolerance — not filed. Caveat: I initially saw jq parse errors suggesting invalid JSON; verified it was my shell's echo mangling backslash escapes, NOT an API fault — all responses re-fetched clean, nothing filed.

Strengths (26/33 OK): named-protocol audit recall is excellent — 19/19 sampled protocols' audits land in top-2 (15 at #1: phoenix 0.87-high, volta, trustless-work, openzeppelin 0.98, grantpicks, capyfi, redstone, equitx, wombat, spiko, aquarius, xycloans, fxdao, soroswap, blend). 'is blend safe to build on' is a model answer: incident doc #1 followed by 5 Blend audits. 'oracle manipulation attack' and 'flash loan attack soroban' both surface the Blend incident + the audits whose own sections discuss those vectors (Alula '7.7 UNAUTHORIZED FEE DRAINING FROM FLASHLOAN', FxDAO '[B7] Only One Oracle Used'). Lane contract is honest: bogus source=hacks → 400 with validSources list; source=incident off-topic q → honest 0. Audit corpus is far richer than the map suggested (~40 protocols, 12+ auditors incl. Cantina, Hacken, Zellic, Quarkslab). Systemic pattern behind the 3 low WRONG-TOPs: on named-entity queries, another protocol's audit outranks the named one ~20% of the time (generic 'audit'-shaped similarity dilutes the entity term; borderline 4th case: SEP-40 Oracle Consumer Interface outranks the Reflector OtterSec audit 0.76-high vs 0.75-high for 'reflector oracle audit' — not filed since the SEP doc's Implementations section is on-entity). Not filed (within top-10 tolerance): 'has stellar ever been hacked' puts 3 unrelated protocol audits above the on-topic CAP-77 quorum-freeze blog (#4) and Blend incident (#5). Coverage caveats: incident source has exactly 1 doc (Blend) so incident-recall breadth is untestable; freshness component is 1.0 with ageDays=null on every audit row regardless of publishedAt (2023 audits get full freshness credit) — no staleness harm demonstrable in this cell, but the field looks uncomputed; audit rows all carry severity='unknown' while the incident carries 'high'.

Corpus freshness itself is GOOD for this cell (weekly roundups through 2026-07-03, USST launch 2026-07-01, P27 Zipper guide 2026-06-04, AMA recap 2026-06-03) — failures are ranking/hygiene, not missing data. Systemic root #1: publishedAt:null → freshness=1.0 (ambiguous-null class); undated dev-docs/SEP/CAP get perfect freshness + 0.95 authority and squat on every latest/news/recent query while dated 2026 news pays the age penalty — flipping null to neutral-or-penalized would fix P1/P3/P7-class failures at once. Root #2: chunk hygiene — dev-docs meeting-note pages indexed as multiple documents (page-doc '2025-04-03' + author-section doc 'Carsten Jacobsen') with no doc-level dedup, so one note burns 2-3 top-K slots; meta's 'best chunk per document' and 'confidence order' claims are both observably false. Root #3: lumenloop-research chunks carry cross-page nav contamination ('View →More from research…4d ago…' trailers embedded in content), which pollutes embeddings and previews. Also noticed: identical query at limit=8 vs limit=12 returned different candidate sets (not a truncation), suggesting candidate-pool size scales with limit — worth a look for pagination stability. OK probes: protocol-27 zipper known-item (top-2), new-stablecoin-launched (USST + roundup in top-5), protocol-upgrade-vote (P27 guide top-8 with defensible how-to rows above). Not touched: bridges/ramps/Etherfuse per anchor discipline.

cells.cell: PARTNERS × NON-RAMP lanes (audit firms, legal, agencies, tooling, infrastructure, wallets, protocols)
cells.notes: Coverage caveats: (1) validTypes advertises legal, agency, on-off-ramp, other — corpus has ZERO records of each; queried directly they return honest 0s with useful routing hints (projects search + POST /api/partners/match), so filed as a data gap, not a retrieval finding: the directory cannot answer 'hire a stellar dev shop' or 'legal counsel' at all. (2) Strengths worth recording: audit-firm lane is excellent — 'audit my smart contract'/'soroban audit'/'formal verification'/'penetration testing'/'zk audit'/'security advisory' all return exactly the service-implied firms with the right #1 (halborn only pentest, veridise only zk, certora/rv/veridise only formal-verification); oracle→reflector, escrow→trustless-work, explorer→stellarexpert, yield vaults→defindex, dex aggregator→soroswap, multisig→lobstr#1, kyc→finclusive all correct; invalid type values 400 with validTypes (self-correcting even for case variants); zero-result responses carry routing hints; q=audit&type=wallet honestly 0. (3) Unfiled tail nits (within tolerance): 'smart contract security review' omits runtime-verification from its 5 results while trustless-work sits at rank 4; 'amm liquidity' ranks anchor-trace-finance above phoenix-protocol (all on-topic AMMs still top-5). (4) accepting filter is non-discriminating (all 44 default partners acceptingClients=true) but truthful. (5) Systemic patterns: default quality bar gates by presentation fields (tagline) not identity — known-item recall failure class (sls-018/019 'structured truth must drive INCLUSION'); validation asymmetry (type strict, sector/region silent); trust lane = another 'uniform confidence' signal like projects.confidence. Ramps lane deliberately untouched per anti-anchoring rule.

Coverage caveat: only 26/114 builders have a non-empty location, so the location lane can only ever surface those 26 — sparsity bounds recall regardless of mapping quality (not filed as a finding; would need external GitHub-profile checks to prove DATA-WRONG). What's healthy: umbrella mapping (africa/europe/latam + 'south america' synonym) works case-insensitively; asia/mena/narnia/usa/india give honest 0s with the filter echoed in meta.filters (no dishonest fallback-to-all); skill= is honestly echoed as q in meta.filters (not silently dropped); q= resolves username, display-name, and project-name lookups exactly; leaderboard ordering is perfectly monotonic vs its stated basis for all 3 sorts (activity/stars/issues) across 50 rows, range=7d genuinely filters (27 rows all in-window), and invalid sort/category/range values return 400 with the valid enum listed — exemplary. Systemic pattern: the skill/q substring matcher is the same no-word-boundary class as the known repos language=java→JavaScript semantics; fixing tokenized/word-boundary matching once would close both. The Brasil/Rio gap is an instance of the endonym/city→country normalization class — same normalizer the partners/anchors coverage fields would need.

cells.cell: LONG-TAIL endpoints (hackathons+rosters, rfps, skills, clusters, analyze, leaderboard, status, changelog)
cells.notes: Coverage: 27 graded probes across all 8 long-tail lanes incl. rosters, compare, detail lanes, bogus-value and filter probes. STRONG overall — this cell is the healthiest surface I've graded: (1) error contract is uniformly excellent — bogus status/dimension/sort/source/slug all return 400/404 with validValues arrays or hints (rfps, hackathons, skills, analyze, leaderboard); (2) currency is real — RFP scfRound block asOf=today with round 45 open (deadline 2026-08-16)/44 notification/43 concluded + verifyAt link, and funding byRound reaches round 44, cross-consistent; /api/status per-source lastUpdatedAt all same-day with honest notes (hackathons 0-curated note, sdfSkills 24h-cache note, EC 'as of 2026-07-01'); changelog current to today; (3) internal consistency — analyze?dimension=hackathons totals (97000 USD / 2861 hackers) exactly equal the sum over /api/hackathons rows; skills bySource sums = 30 = returned; leaderboard rank strictly follows the declared sort. Hackathon rosters rich (ZK: 345 submissions; winners present with isWinner+award on all events older than ~1 month). NOT filed: stellar-hacks-zk 0 winners (ended 2026-07-03, judging plausibly pending upstream — 3 older events all have winners); byRound missing rounds 4/5/12 (plausibly no surviving directory project from those rounds, not provable); 'nethermind' on leaderboard (they built the Stellar RISC-Zero verifier — plausibly tracking Stellar-specific repos). Systemic pattern: the two medium findings are both cross-lane aggregate honesty — clusters lacks the scope self-labeling analyze already has (fix = one meta note or align scope), and the projects.hackathon linkage field is null corpus-wide, zeroing three analytics surfaces the roster data could populate (same structured-truth-drives-inclusion class as sls-018/019).

Coverage: 15 wrong-lane routing probes (code/spec/hackathon/people/product/stats/RFP/learning/audit-firm/leaderboard questions aimed at projects, repos, research, partners, repos/explain) + 15 unknown-param/enum/zero-result honesty probes across projects, repos, research, partners, builders, analyze. 5 auxiliary verification fetches (builders location enumeration, hackathons list+detail, status, leaderboard) grounded answerability claims. No bridge/ramp/Etherfuse probes used. STRONG POSITIVES worth preserving: 400-with-valid-enum on projects?category, partners?type, research?source (+hint field), analyze?dimension, research missing-q (with example URL); builders 0-result advisory ('filter miss, not an empty directory' + recovery channels); partners meta (validTypes/validRamps/filteredOut + capability-fit note + hints on empty); projects no_query advisory documenting q aliases; repos/explain honest answered:false + recovery note; repos honest 0 for unknown language. Coerced values (scfAwarded=banana→false, minScore=abc→0) are disclosed via meta.filters echo — acceptable, but a warnings[] array would make silent coercion visible to non-echo-reading agents. SYSTEMIC PATTERN: cross-lane hints exist per-lane but are inconsistent — projects: keyword-triggered rich 4-lane block even with results; repos/partners: empty-results-only, narrower vocab; research: none; builders: advisory. One shared hints module with the full lane map (incl. hackathons/rfps/status/leaderboard/skills) triggered on low-relevance (not just empty) would close findings 3-5. DATA-GAP for another cell: /api/hackathons records (list AND ?id= detail) carry NO winners field (keys end at prizePoolUSD/status/url) while 'who won X' is a golden-question class — winners live only on project.hackathonPlacement, so no lane cleanly answers winner queries today. Builders location is free text ('Recife. Brazil. Earth.'); location=Nigeria honestly returns 0 (no Nigerian builders among the 26 with location set, 88 null).

Retrieval via prose is the healthy half: 19/21 graded capability probes OK — secondary products named in descriptions DO retrieve their records (deva wallet #1, block explorer rumble-fish #3, virtual card cryptomate #3/mercuryo #4, iban thexbank #2, payroll tumbl #8, tax ledgerstax #2, voting stellot #7, orderbook axis #2, risk oracle untangled #1, payment cards venerez #4/thexbank #5, contract registry #1, smart account the-aha-company #10, cetes bando #2, australian dollar stablecoin novatti #1, price oracle reflector #1, control non-custodial wallet all-valid top-10). The systemic gap is the STRUCTURED lane: types[]=[] on ~9% of sampled records (7/75: rumble-fish, novatti, reflector, ledgerstax, cryptomate, automated-finance, noir, the-aha-company, backyard) including flagship multi-product ones, so any consumer filtering/weighting by types misses their capabilities entirely — same class as the F1 structured-fields root in the 597-probe audit. Not filed (fair-grading calls): stellot types=[SDK] for a voting platform (taxonomy has no Voting type, SDK unprovably wrong); humantech absent from crowded q=identity top-10 (all 10 above it are identity-primary projects); stellarport outranked in 69-strong non-custodial wallet crowd (ordering taste). Anchoring guard respected: no bridge/ramp/Etherfuse-focused probes (cetes touched etherfuse #1 naturally). Coverage caveat: sampled 75 records across 5 categories at random offsets; Asset/Anchor sampled lightly except the full 32-row Asset null-desc scan; Partner Integration (n=2) untouched.

cells.cell: DATA-TRUTH spot-verify (12+ random Live projects, liveness vs own linked artifacts)
cells.notes: Method: seeded proportional random sampling (python random.seed 20260709/20260710) via category+offset probes — 7 User-Facing App, 4 Infrastructure, 3 Tooling, 2 Protocol/Contract, 1 Asset, 1 Anchor = 18 graded Live records; each verified against its OWN served artifacts (links.website via HEAD + GET-with-browser-UA fallback + parked-domain grep, repo lastCommitAt in the row, description vs status). okCount=16 = 15 sampled records fully clean + 1 opportunistic side-verify (usdx/fxdao.io suspected dead from prior session memory but verified ALIVE, HTTP 200 'Hello from FxDAO' — deliberately NOT filed; fair-grading). Grading discipline applied: stale repo alone not flagged per stale-repo≠defunct policy (stellargpt repo last commit 2023-10 but site serves a real Next.js app = OK; k3-labs, qolaq-class distinguished by the SITE being dead, not the repo). gateway.fm HEAD→403 but GET→200 (bot block, not dead — HEAD-only liveness checks would false-positive). Systemic patterns: (1) both true liveness failures are prominence-0 long-tail records, suggesting no automated dead-site sweep over links.website exists — a cheap HTTP-liveness guard (404/NXDOMAIN/NO_HOST_ENTRY detection) would catch the qolaq class corpus-wide; (2) zusd shows Asset records need issuer wind-down/redemption-only detection — 'Live' for a stablecoin is a high-stakes claim agents repeat; (3) minor unfiled nits: zusd and 5x-crypto have empty shortDescription; 5x-crypto's only website link is a YouTube channel (alive). Ok-rate for this cell: 15/18 sampled = 83% clean on data-truth liveness.

confirmed.expected: A query matching zero keywords should signal that honestly: matchMode reflecting the semantic fallback, low/appropriate confidence, and a real total. (Fonbnk exists 1 edit away, so ideally fuzzy-rescue; at minimum don't claim a keyword match.)
confirmed.observed: 15 unrelated projects (veur, boss-pay, kes, wallet-guru, pakana...) served with meta.matchMode:'strict' + matchModeLabel:'all keywords matched' (false — zero keywords matched; every hit is via:'semantic'), confidence 0.89–0.97 'high' on all rows, and counts.total fabricated to equal limit (10→15→25 as limit grows). Reproduced identically on q=kurra, q=almanac, q=certick, q=tezorro. An agent asking about a slightly-misspelled brand receives a confident, fully-wrong answer set.

verdict.evidence:
CONFIRMED — reproduced 2026-07-09 on live stellarlight.xyz; the contrast controls prove the meta is false, not correct-but-different.

1) Primary probe re-run: /api/projects/search?q=fonbank&limit=15 → meta: {"matchMode":"strict","matchModeLabel":"all keywords matched","semantic":true,"counts":{"returned":15,"total":15}}. All 15 rows are via:"semantic" (VEUR, Boss Pay, KES, Wallet Guru, Pakana, Honey Coin, ...), every row confidence.label "high" with score 0.89-0.95 and relevance 0.96-1.0 (VEUR relevance 1.0 for query "fonbank"). Zero keyword matches, yet the label asserts "all keywords matched".

2) The label demonstrably means keyword matching elsewhere, so it is false here: q=wallet&limit=5 → same matchMode "strict" / "all keywords matched" but semantic:false, rows via:"keyword" (Lobstr, xBull, Freighter), and counts {"returned":5,"total":160} — a REAL corpus-wide total. q=fonbnk (correct spelling) → 1 result Fonbnk via:"keyword", semantic:false, total 1. So Fonbnk exists 1 edit away (claim premise fair) and never appears anywhere in fonbank's top-25 (checked; ranks 16-25 are Elroy, Hi Fifo, Benkiko, ... — no Fonbnk).

3) counts.total fabrication confirmed: q=fonbank at limit=10/15/25 → total=10/15/25, tracking limit exactly; genuine keyword queries report real totals (wallet: 160). An agent cannot distinguish "15 of 15 matched" from padded fallback.

4) Breadth confirmed: q=kurra, certick, tezorro → identical pattern (matchMode strict, "all keywords matched", semantic:true, all rows via semantic, all confidence "high" 0.87-0.95, total==returned==15). q=almanac same but total=4 (still total==returned). Garbage control q=zzqqxxplumbus → 15 "high"-confidence (0.90-0.92) semantic results, "all keywords matched", total 15 — pure noise served as a confident full match, so there is no floor.

One mitigating nuance (does not refute): meta DOES contain a bare "semantic":true boolean that flips only on fallback (false for wallet/fonbnk), and rows carry via:"semantic" — a careful agent could infer fallback. But the designated mode fields (matchMode "strict" + matchModeLabel "all keywords matched") make a literally false assertion right next to it, confidence labels are "high" and numerically indistinguishable from exact-brand keyword hits (misspelled-fallback VEUR 0.92 == exact Fonbnk 0.92; wallet keyword hits 0.92-0.97), and counts.total is only non-real in exactly the fallback case. Net: the response affirmatively signals a confident keyword match that did not happen. EMPTY-DISHONEST stands (empty keyword-match state disguised as a full confident match); fix should flip matchMode/matchModeLabel to a semantic-fallback value, cap/re-band fallback confidence, and report total honestly (0 keyword matches, N semantic suggestions).

confirmed.expected: One canonical record for this entity: zkliquid's own shortDescription states 'ZKLiquid has rebranded to LiquidsFi (liquids.fi)' and both records carry the identical website https://liquids.fi/ — so zkliquid should be canonicalized to liquidsfi (canonicalSlug) or merged.
confirmed.observed: Two separate Live 'Bridge' records — slug 'liquidsfi' (rank 0) and slug 'zkliquid' (rank 1) — both canonicalSlug:null, presented as two distinct projects for one protocol.

verdict.evidence:
CONFIRMED — reproduced and the expectation is the system's own documented contract, not auditor taste.

1) Probe re-run (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=liquidsfi&limit=10, 2026-07-09): returns counts {returned:2, total:2} — slug "liquidsfi" (rank 0) and slug "zkliquid" (rank 1), BOTH status:"Live", BOTH types:["Bridge"], BOTH canonicalSlug:null, lifecycle:null.

2) Same entity, provable from the records' own data: zkliquid's shortDescription opens "ZKLiquid has rebranded to LiquidsFi (liquids.fi)." and the two records carry byte-identical links — website "https://liquids.fi/", github "https://github.com/liquidsfi", twitter "https://x.com/liquidsfi". The liquidsfi record's repo list even includes "liquidsfi/ZKLiquid-protocol", i.e. the repos are already merged under liquidsfi.

3) Expectation fairness: the live OpenAPI spec (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/openapi.json) documents the exact mechanism: canonicalSlug = "When this record is a known duplicate/rename, the slug of the CANONICAL record to prefer; null for canonical records themselves. Follow it before citing counts or funding." So a known rename with canonicalSlug:null on both records violates the API's own contract; DATA-WRONG (structured field missing vs the record's own prose) is the right label.

4) Not correct-but-different behavior: (a) keeping an alias record for searchability is the spec's canonicalSlug pattern — the field is null; (b) corpus norm for renames is ONE record (tricorn/Utexo: q=utexo and q=rebranded both return only slug "tricorn", no separate utexo record), making this two-Live-record pair an outlier; (c) nothing distinguishes them historically — both scfAwarded:false, no hackathon/awards — so no attribution reason for two records.

5) Consumer impact is real, not stylistic: reverse known-item probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=zkliquid&limit=10 returns ONLY zkliquid (total:1, Live, canonicalSlug:null) — an agent gets a distinct "Live" protocol with no machine-readable dedupe pointer at all, and counts double one protocol as two.

Side observation (context, not this finding): canonicalSlug appears unused corpus-wide (0 of 100 sampled Protocol/Contract records set it; the tricorn record's "formerly Tricorn" also null) — the field may be defined but never populated anywhere.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran both probes 2026-07-09. (1) https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=stellarexpert&limit=5 → 3 results: #1 slug 'stellarexpert' (name "StellarExpert", types ["Explorer","Analytics"], links.website "https://stellar.expert/", status "Live", canonicalSlug null), #2 axis, #3 slug 'stellar-expert' (name "Stellar Expert", types ["Analytics"], links.website "https://stellar.expert/", status "Live", canonicalSlug null, shortDescription begins "StellarExpert is a comprehensive block explorer and analytics platform..."). Same entity provable from the rows' own data: identical website, same GitHub org (stellar-expert org vs stellar-expert/stellar-expert-explorer repo), and the 'stellar-expert' record's prose names itself "StellarExpert". Both Live, both canonicalSlug:null, lifecycle:null — no dedup marker despite canonicalSlug existing in the response schema. (2) https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=stellar%20expert&limit=13 → 13 results: #1 stellar-expert, #2 beamable, #3 tender, #4 axis, ... #11 stellarexpert — the flagship record is absent from top-5 as claimed (in fact rank 11/13, behind prose-matches on the word "expert" like Beamable/Tender/TEACHMEDEFI). Identity facets are split across the two rows (Explorer type + explorer repo only on 'stellarexpert'; twitter + org-level repos only on 'stellar-expert'), so scores/attributes for one entity are fragmented and which partial row you get depends on query spacing. The claim's observed evidence reproduces verbatim; the expectation (one canonical row or canonicalSlug linkage) is fair and grounded in the records' own data.

verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED. (1) Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=stablecoins+on+stellar&limit=10 — reproduces exactly as claimed: #1 allbridge types=[Bridge] prom 88, #2 defindex [Lending] 78, #3 circle [Stablecoin] 90, #4 moneygram [Anchor], #5 ondo [RWA], #6 yellow-card [Anchor], #7 glo-dollar [Stablecoin], #8 fonbnk [Anchor], #9 brale [Stablecoin], #10 spacewalk [Bridge] → 7/10 off-type, only 3 Stablecoin-typed. (2) Corpus expectation verified: q=stablecoin&limit=148 shows exactly 30 types=[Stablecoin] Live records (circle 90, brale 72, glo-dollar 66, usdc, ngnc, stablecorp, qcad, ars, brz...). (3) The expected top-K is provably fair because the SYSTEM ITSELF produces it on the singular form: q=stablecoin+on+stellar → total 148, top-10 = 10/10 Stablecoin-typed with circle #1 (then glo-dollar, brale, coopstable, ars, brl, pen, usdc, glousd, usdx). (4) Root cause isolated: plural 'stablecoins' doesn't stem to 'stablecoin' — q=stablecoins alone reproduces the identical broken top-10 (total drops 148→81, silently excluding 25/30 on-type records incl. usdc/ngnc), and ranking among surviving prose-matches falls back to TVL/prominence with no type signal (allbridge tvl $20.28M > defindex $16.39M > circle tvl null despite prom 90). (5) Not correct-but-different: no type filter lane exists — ?type=Stablecoin is silently ignored (not echoed in meta.filters, 0 results), so q= is the only browse lane; and allbridge is served with confidence relevance:1.0 label:'high' on this query, compounding the mislead. WRONG-TOP label stands (off-type outranks on-type within the match set); note an underlying RECALL-MISS component also exists (25/30 on-type records absent from the plural match set due to missing stemming), worth filing as the mechanism.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe 2026-07-09: q=payment+apps+on+stellar&limit=10 → top-10 slugs (slug/types/prominence): digitpay-finance/Payments/0, stride/AI-Gaming-NFT-Payments/0, socketfi/SDK-Payments-Security/0, paychant/Payments/0, paysapp/Payments/0, typescript-wallet-sdk/SDK/70, bitget-wallet/Wallet/60, lumexo/Wallet/0, rehive/(no type)/0, planet-pay/SDK/0 — all 5 flagships absent, type-match 5/10, off-type rows exactly as claimed. Control q=payments+on+stellar → ranks 1-5 = beans(70), decaf(66), stellar-disbursement-platform-sdp(72), felix-pago(70), chipper(68) — exactly the expected 5. Ground-truth check: filtering a 300-row sample on records' own types+prominence gives sdp 72, beans 70, felix-pago 70, chipper 68, decaf 66, then a cliff to prominence 0 — so the expected set is provably the data's own top-5 Payments records, and the claimed prominence values are all correct. Tolerance fair: 251 total matches = crowded → top-10. ONE MECHANISM CORRECTION: not hard AND-exclusion — full fetch (limit=251) shows the 5 flagships at ranks 11-15 with score:1, while every top-10 row has score:2 (text contains literal plural 'apps', e.g. digitpay 'web and mobile apps'; none of the 5 flagships' text contains 'apps' — beans/felix/chipper have singular 'app' only, no plural normalization). So the generic-noun token suppresses them below the cut via keyword-count-dominates-prominence/type ranking rather than removing them from the set; the top-10 effect is identical to the claim. Side finding: meta reports matchMode:'strict'/'all keywords matched' yet the set contains score-1 rows (decaf, sdp) matching neither 'apps' nor 'app' — the meta label is inaccurate.
verdict.correctedMode: RECALL-MISS label correct as filed; only the parenthetical mechanism needs refining (rank-suppression by 'apps' keyword-count score 2>1, flagships at ranks 11-15, not strict-AND set-exclusion)

verdict.evidence:
CONFIRMED — re-ran all probes 2026-07-09T23:05Z, reproduces exactly.

Main probe: GET https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=wallets+on+stellar&limit=10 (matchMode "strict", all score=1, total=140) returned: #1 lobstr (Wallet, 90), #2 xbull (Wallet, 90), #3 warmancer (Gaming/NFT/AI, 82), #4 dfns (Security, 80), #5 beamable (Gaming/SDK, 80), #6 defindex (Lending, 78), #7 freighter (Wallet, 90), #8 hana (Wallet, 76), #9 stellarterm (DEX, 72), #10 beans (Wallet/Payments, 70). That is 5/10 off-type per the records' own `types` field, and freighter — SDF's official wallet, types=[Wallet], prominence 90 — sits at #7 below a prominence-82 game.

Off-type records match only via incidental prose: warmancer "on-chain items/abilities tied to your wallet" (a strategy game); beamable "SDKs add Stellar wallet login" (game backend); defindex "let wallets, neobanks, and fintechs offer stablecoin savings" (wallets are its customers, not what it is); dfns "MPC wallet-as-a-service" (B2B key-management infra, types=[Security] — the only arguably wallet-adjacent one, but still typed non-Wallet by its own data).

Albedo displacement confirmed: with limit=40, Wallet-typed positions are 1,2,7,8,10,11,14,15,20,21… — albedo (Wallet, 80) is #11, outside top-10, while beamable (80, off-type) holds #5; rabet (Wallet, 78) #14 and vesseo (Wallet, 78) #15 rank below defindex (Lending, 78) #6. Ranking demonstrably ignores the types field on this phrasing.

Corpus support: q=wallet&limit=200 → 56 Wallet-typed records among first 100 matches (claim's "56" figure exact), so plenty of on-type candidates existed to fill a top-10.

Control: GET /api/projects/search?q=wallet&limit=10 → 10/10 Wallet-typed (lobstr, xbull, freighter, hana, beans, albedo, rabet, vesseo, solar-wallet, stellar-passport). Minor claim inaccuracy, immaterial: this control is NOT in strict prominence order (hana 76 and beans 70 above albedo 80) — but the substance (single-word query is fully type-coherent, multi-word natural phrasing collapses to type-blind ranking) holds.

Fairness check passed: "wallets on stellar" is the canonical natural browse phrasing an agent (Raven) sends; expected top-K derives from the structured types field, not taste; top-10 tolerance for a crowded 56-record type is generous, and this is not equal-validity reordering — Gaming/Security/Lending records are provably not wallets by their own data. No correct-but-different explanation found: all results score=1 in the same keyword lane, so the off-type ranking isn't a relevance-score artifact, and freighter below warmancer also violates prominence order, ruling out "prominence-ordered by design".

RECALL-MISS label is correct; disclosed matchMode meta makes it honest-but-under-recalling, not EMPTY-DISHONEST.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe 2026-07-09 (GET https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=anchors+on+stellar&limit=10, matchMode=strict, total=39). Observed ranking reproduces the claim exactly: #1 scorechain (types Security/Analytics, category User-Facing App, prominence 78), #2 circle (Stablecoin, 90), #3 moneygram (Anchor, 90), #4 bitso (Anchor, 84), #5 didstellar (Security, 80), #6 yellow-card (Anchor, 80), #7 fonbnk (Anchor, 76), #8 typescript-wallet-sdk (SDK, 70), #9 chipper (Payments, 68), #10 mykobo (Anchor, 0) — type-match 5/10, only 2 anchors in top-5. Expectation verified as fair from the data itself: enumerating every category, exactly 25 projects have "Anchor" in types, and the only 5 with prominence>0 are precisely the claimed expected set — moneygram 90, bitso 84, yellow-card 80, etherfuse 78 (types RWA/Anchor), fonbnk 76. Root cause visible in the records: scorechain matches only via prose ("gives Stellar anchors and institutions the transaction-monitoring and compliance tooling") and circle via prose ("serve as base settlement currency across Stellar anchors"); their own types fields say non-anchor, yet prose mentions outrank structured type matches at equal/lower prominence — not reordering among equals, so WRONG-TOP stands. Stronger than claimed: etherfuse is absent from the FULL 39-result set (limit=39 scan, no rank), because its shortDescription never contains "anchor" ("issues Stablebonds… operates Etherfuse FX, a Mexico fiat on/off-ramp API") — proving the types array is not searched at all (structured-field-not-inclusion, F1 class); prominence-0 prose-matching anchors (mykobo #10, anclap #14) rank while a prominence-78 Anchor-typed record can't match. Correct-but-different check: category=Anchor filter exists but keys off the coarse `category` field (19 records) and ALSO misses etherfuse (category=Protocol/Contract), so no lane rescues the natural-language query. Two corrections to the claim record: (1) "36 Anchor-typed records" is wrong — 25 in the projects lane (partners lane has 27 anchor-type partners, the likely conflation); (2) the etherfuse-absence facet is strictly RECALL-MISS (absent from all results, not outranked) layered on the primary WRONG-TOP, which remains the right label for scorechain/circle above all anchors.

verdict.evidence: REPRODUCED (2026-07-09T23:07Z): GET https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=learn+stellar+development&limit=10 → #4 stellar-pulse (via=semantic, score 0.7367, types=["Analytics"], shortDescription:null, prominence:null) and #5 stellar-dashboard (semantic 0.7131, Analytics, shortDescription:null), ranking above #7 dapp-world (Education, "Comprehensive learning track on Stellar to help developers onboard...", 0.7019), #8 stellarprodev ("Stellar-focused education platform for post-beginner developers...", 0.6966), #9 stellar-update (Education, 0.6950) — same semantic lane, so this is score misordering, not lane artifact. FAIRNESS CHECK PASSES: the outranked records' own data (types=Education + learning-track descriptions) directly implies the query; the two empty rows' own data (Analytics types, zero text; stellar-dashboard = dashboard.stellar.org network-stats page) does not — off-topic+content-free above on-topic+content-rich is provable misordering, not taste. CONTENT-FREE CONFIRMED AT SOURCE: the public detail page https://stellarlight.xyz/project/stellar-pulse renders "About Stellar Pulse — No description available." — a true data gap, not a search-payload omission. CORPUS COUNT VERIFIED: paged all 7 categories (limit=100 × offsets) → 892 unique served records, 59 with empty shortDescription (6.6%); status split 57 Live / 1 Development / 1 Inactive (claim's "59/892 Live" is right in aggregate, trivially imprecise on the split — 2 of the 59 are non-Live). All 5 named examples (steexp, blockeden, baf-nework, give-credit, stellar-carbon) confirmed empty. AGGRAVATOR: the API serves confidence {score:0.92, label:"high", relevance:1.0} on the content-free stellar-pulse row for this query — the confidence layer actively tells agents an unusable row is a perfect answer. REFUTATION ATTEMPTS THAT FAILED TO KILL IT: (1) empty rows do NOT pollute broadly — 3 control queries (stellar remittances africa / soroban contract auditing / network health monitoring) show zero empty-desc rows in top-8, so this is query-conditional, but the filed probe stands; (2) stellar-dashboard appearing via keyword at #7 on "stellar analytics dashboard" is legitimate (its name/types imply that query) — the defect is specifically empty rows semantic-matching a learning query at 0.71-0.74. Mode note: JUNK-ROW stands (row served is content-free to the consuming agent); root cause is DATA-WRONG-adjacent (missing shortDescription field on 59 records) and the probe manifestation has WRONG-TOP character (off-topic empty rows above Education rows).

verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED — reproduced, control-tested, differential-tested, and root-caused. (1) Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=SEP-30&limit=5: 3 results (TZS, PEN, MXNe — Asset/Stablecoin stubs, shortDescription:null, anchorProfile:null, no SEP data anywhere in their served records), all via:"semantic" with raw scores 0.601–0.607, yet confidence = {score:0.92,label:"high",relevance:1,freshness:1} (TZS), {0.97,"high",1,1} (PEN), {0.91,"high",0.99,1} (MXNe). Claimed range 0.92–0.97 is actually 0.91–0.97 — immaterial. SEP-30 is account recovery; nothing in these fiat-stablecoin records implies it, and via:"semantic" proves no keyword hit, so "fallback near-miss" is a fair characterization grounded in the records' own data. (2) Control q=Blend: exact keyword matches at score 1.0 get confidence {0.97,"high",relevance:1} — byte-identical trust to the SEP-30 noise, so the signal cannot distinguish confident hits from fallback noise, exactly as claimed. (3) Differential q=quantum%20yoga%20retreat (zero plausible relevance): 5 semantic fallbacks (scores 0.613–0.628) ALL served confidence 0.90–0.96 "high", relevance 0.98–1 — non-discrimination holds for arbitrary nonsense. (4) Root cause (read-only source): src/lib/confidence.ts projectConfidence() line 177-178 computes relevance = score/maxScore relative to the returned set with no ScoreMode awareness, and src/app/api/projects/search/route.ts:191-201 feeds raw semantic cosines in — so the top of ANY result set gets relevance=1 by construction and the "relevance < 0.2 → cap at 0.4" guardrail can never fire for it. The expectation is not auditor taste: the same file's vector normalization used for /api/research — relevanceFrom(): "(score - 0.55) / 0.3 … so confidence actually discriminates instead of hugging the top" — applied to TZS's 0.607 yields relevance 0.19 → guardrail caps composite at 0.4 → label "low", which is precisely the claim's expected behavior per the module's own documented contract ("a barely-relevant result can't be 'trustworthy'"). Note freshness:1 comes from status:"Live" proxy (documented design, defensible alone) — the defect is the relevance leg. Adjacent aggravator observed in passing: the same SEP-30 response's meta says matchMode:"strict"/matchModeLabel:"all keywords matched" while every row is via:"semantic". Mode label DATA-WRONG stands (a served structured trust field false vs the match-quality reality served beside it); cell PROJECTS × confidence-signal correct.

verdict.evidence: All probes re-run 2026-07-09T23:13Z; every observation reproduces, and one stronger datum surfaced. (1) Control is real: /api/projects/search?q=biggest%20dex fires meta.superlativeNote ("Superlative detected: result ORDER here reflects relevance + curated prominence + liveness — NOT a ranking by size, usage, or fees..."). Word-list characterization shows the gap is systematic, not a mode artifact: "most popular wallet" (strict) FIRES, "top wallets" (strict) FIRES, "largest projects" (majority) FIRES — but "newest dex" (strict), "latest projects" (strict), and "newest projects" (semantic) all return superlativeNote:null. So the guard fires in both strict and majority modes for size words and never for temporal superlatives. (2) Hints on both temporal probes list only repos/research/partners/builders — no leaderboard entry exists in the hint catalog, while /api/leaderboard?sort=activity works exactly as claimed (rank-1 Microvault lastActivityAt 2026-07-06, 3 days old; validSorts=[activity,stars,issues]). One caveat for the fixer: leaderboard sort=activity means "recently active" (GitHub), not "recently launched" — it is the closest recency lane, not a perfect answer, so the ideal fix is temporal-superlative guard text (order/inclusion is keyword-based, corpus has no launch-date field) plus a sort=activity signpost, and the Spectra negation-match also implicates the known omission=negation prose-matching class.

(1) Failing probe re-run: /api/projects/search?q=protect%20my%20savings%20from%20inflation&limit=10 → "matchMode":"loose-1","counts":{"returned":1,"total":1}, sole result sava (score 2). Both dropped records contain the query's operative token in their OWN data: vesseo "Widely adopted across high-inflation LatAm markets (~93% Argentina)... buy, store, send, receive, and spend digital dollars"; depay "Enabling crypto payments in inflation-hit economies". (4) Mechanism probes prove phrasing fragility, not intended semantics: the relaxation ladder (strict→loose-1→majority) stops at the FIRST tier with any hit. (1) Probe re-run (q=how%20do%20i%20earn%20yield%20on%20stellar&limit=52): blend #1, warmancer #3, beamable #4, stellar-unity-developer-kit #7, nebulavrf #8 (all conf 0.97, score 2); defindex and hedgepay ABSENT from all 52 — matches the claim verbatim. verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=can%20i%20buy%20stocks%20on%20stellar&limit=10 — exact reproduction: ranks 1-3 on-topic via keyword (equitx conf 0.65 medium/rel 0.5; investar 0.60; stocken-capital 0.60), ranks 4-6 SDK-typed semantic backfill with HIGHER confidence (kotlin-stellar-sdk 0.92 high/rel 1.0; stellar-nest 0.91/0.99; elixir-stellar-sdk 0.91/0.98) despite types=["SDK"] and descriptions ("Interact with Stellar using idiomatic Kotlin") containing zero equities content. Confidence claim CONFIRMED and contract-violating: live OpenAPI documents confidence as "Blended confidence for this result... decomposed into relevance/freshness/authority", and src/lib/confidence.ts composeConfidence docstring says "One scale + label set across endpoints so an agent reads confidence the same way everywhere" — but relevance is normalized per-lane (top semantic hit always rel≈1.0 vs its own lane max; keyword hits in matchMode=majority get flat rel=0.5), inverting the trust signal across lanes. Mechanism generalizes: control probe ?q=where%20can%20i%20get%20a%20loan%20on%20stellar returns 10 keyword hits — exact-fit Lending projects (blend, lantern, airswift) all capped at flat rel=0.5/conf 0.60-0.65 medium — so ANY query where semantic backfill fires will show off-topic backfill labeled "high" above on-topic "medium" keyword hits.
verdict.correctedMode: DATA-WRONG (served confidence/relevance field is false vs the record's own data and contradicts the endpoint's own score ordering; per-lane normalization artifact). Not WRONG-TOP — positional ranking is correct, on-topic records hold ranks 1-3.

Additional evidence widening the class: orbit-finance at #25 on the same query is a third empty-description row (conf 0.79, still labeled "high") — the confidence scorer does not penalize zero-content records. verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=learn%20about%20crypto%20for%20beginners&limit=10 — reproduces exactly: total=5, ranks = cryptomate (keyword, conf 0.75), dogstar (keyword, 0.75), honey-coin (semantic, conf 0.97, relevance 1.0), splito (semantic, 0.96), crypto-link (semantic, 0.91); stellar-quest and venalabs absent from the ENTIRE result set (tolerance moot). Expectation verified from records' own data: venalabs has structured types:['Education'] + desc "Web3 education platform with hands-on Stellar and Soroban courses... project-based learning"; stellar-quest desc "gamifies learning about the Stellar network"; both Live. honey-coin verified off-topic: types:['Payments'], desc "African fiat-to-stablecoin anchor... USDC/USDT on/off-ramps across 18+ markets via mobile money and bank rails" — zero education content yet scored relevance 1.0. Refutation attempts failed: (a) not a coverage gap — corpus has a ~10-record education class (soroban-learn, edunode, easya, 5x-crypto and onboarding-club both types:['Education'], block-by-block...), none surfaced; (b) not missing embeddings — keyword-free semantic probe ?q=where%20can%20a%20newcomer%20study%20blockchain%20basics retrieves the education class via semantic (teachmedefi rel 1.0, edunode 0.98, soroban-learn 0.96, easya 0.96), so the miss is query-specific semantic scoring; honey-coin ALSO false-positives at #9 on that probe (recurring over-matching embedding); (c) not correct-but-different — even a consumer-product reading can't justify a B2B remittance anchor at rel 1.0 for a "learn about" query. Keyword-lane absence is explainable (loose-1 requires ~2 of {learn,crypto,beginners}; both expected descs lack "crypto"/"beginners"), so this phrasing depends wholly on the semantic lane, which failed. Confidence inversion confirmed: off-topic semantic 0.97 > on-topic keyword 0.75. Minor calibration: the named pair is one pick from the broader education class — the provable failure is class-level absence, which strengthens the finding. RECALL-MISS label is correct.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=games%20i%20can%20play%20on%20stellar&limit=12 — order reproduces exactly as claimed: warmancer #1, beamable #2, kale #3, stellar-unity-developer-kit #4, nebulavrf #5, cyberbrawl #7, token-tails #10 (12 total). The expectation is grounded in the records' own structured fields, not taste: beamable is category="Tooling", types=["Gaming","SDK"], desc "game server and LiveOps platform... Core gaming backend SDK"; stellar-unity-developer-kit is Tooling/["SDK","Gaming"] "Unity SDK for building blockchain games"; nebulavrf is Tooling/["Gaming","NFT","SDK"] "Verifiable Random Function... core GameFi primitive". The outranked records self-identify as playable consumer games: cyberbrawl User-Facing App "Players battle in real-time duels and tournaments... A live Stellar GameFi title"; token-tails User-Facing App "play-to-save game where players adopt virtual NFT cats"; kale User-Facing App, pure ["Gaming"], prominence 90, "The canonical Stellar on-chain game". Mechanism confirmed: every result has flat score=2 (matchMode=strict, semantic=false), so ranking is entirely tiebreak = prominence + an SCF-award boost of >10 pts (beamable prom 80 + SCF $150K, authority 0.85 outranks kale prom 90, no SCF, authority 0.6; zero-prominence SCF winners findtruman/token-tails outrank zero-prominence non-SCF kalien/cryptocannoneer). Not refutable as correct-but-different: the category facet exists for callers, but the tooling records are not equally-valid answers to explicit play intent per their own category/type fields. Root cause is the known authority-ranking class (SCF boost dominating tiebreak), same as project_repo_search_relevance_defect. Severity as filed (mild/low) is right: top-1 is a playable game and all playable games are within top-10 of 12 — ordering-only, no recall miss.
verdict.correctedMode: WRONG-TOP (label correct)

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=most+used+wallet&limit=10 (2026-07-09T23:16Z): reproduces exactly. #1 Sava {"types":[],"prominence":0,"confidence":{"score":0.97,"label":"high","relevance":1}}, #2 SocketFi {"types":["SDK","Payments","Security"],"prominence":0,"confidence":0.97 high}, then Lobstr/xBull/Freighter {"types":["Wallet"],"prominence":90} at #3-5 with confidence 0.60-0.65 "medium", relevance 0.5 — confidence anti-correlates with the records' own structured types. Fairness verified: 56 Wallet-typed records exist (q=wallet&limit=100), and the engine itself grades Lobstr/xBull/Freighter 0.92-0.97 "high" on the plain "wallet" query, so its own ground truth contradicts the superlative ranking. Mechanism pinned: Sava's prose contains "most" once ("Most stop at the exchange and miss out") and "wallet" incidentally, but NOT "used"; both q=most+used+wallet and q=most+popular+wallet put Sava #1 at score 2 vs wallets' score 1 — the superlative adjective is dropped but the bare token "most" is kept as a content keyword, doubling keyword score → relevance 1.0 → confidence 0.97. Control probe q=best+wallet ranks correctly (Lobstr 0.97, xBull 0.97, Freighter 0.92 at top-3), isolating the residual-"most"-token as the cause. Refutations rejected: the superlativeNote disclaimer warns order ≠ usage ranking but itself claims order reflects "relevance + curated prominence" — violated (prominence-0 rows on top), and per-row confidence 0.97 still steers an agent to Sava; Sava (TRY savings app, empty types) is not an equally-valid answer so this is not ordering taste; recall is intact (wallets at #3-10) so WRONG-TOP, not RECALL-MISS, is the right label. Minor secondary note: SocketFi's prose self-describes as "smart wallet dApp" while types lack Wallet — possible adjacent DATA-WRONG on its types, but does not rescue the finding.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran both probes 2026-07-09. (1) /api/projects/search?q=blend+vs+yieldblox&limit=8 → counts {returned:3,total:3}: Yieldblox (keyword, score 2), Blox Global (semantic 0.70), Backyard (semantic 0.69) — Blend absent from the ENTIRE result set. Mechanism confirmed: matchMode="strict" ("all keywords matched"); Yieldblox's own prose contains "Blend" ("built on the Blend Protocol... one of its Blend lending pools was drained...") so it passes strict-AND, while Blend's record lacks "yieldblox" and is excluded; semantic fallback admits fuzz rows (Blox Global, Backyard) but still not Blend. (2) /api/projects/search?q=aquarius+vs+soroswap&limit=8 → 8 rows (Soroswap #1, StellarBroker, Orion, Dig, WOWMAX, Sorosorcerer, Sorscan, Sorosan); Aquarius absent from all 8, yet q=aquarius returns Aquarius #1 (tvl $43.6M, prominence 85), and Soroswap's shortDescription contains "Aquarius" ("routes trades across Soroban AMMs (Soroswap, Phoenix, Aquarius)") — asymmetric strict-AND exactly as claimed. No signpost/note field appears in meta for comparison queries. Expectation is ground-truth-fair (both names verbatim in query; tolerance generous — Blend missing from a 3-row total, Aquarius from 8). Not explainable as correct-but-different behavior: off-topic semantic rows are included while the named record is not. RECALL-MISS is the correct label (inclusion failure, not ranking preference); same strict-AND class as sls-018/019.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=leading+dex&limit=8 (2026-07-09T23:18Z). Reproduces almost exactly: top-8 = 1 zkCross (DEX, prom 0, conf 0.97), 2 Bitso (Anchor, prom 84, conf 0.92), 3 SubQuery (Indexer, prom 0, conf 0.97), 4 Matrixdock (RWA, prom 0, conf 0.92), 5 Soroswap (DEX, prom 90, conf 0.65/relevance 0.5), 6 Aquarius (DEX, prom 85, conf 0.65), 7 Phoenix (DEX, prom 80), 8 StellarBroker (DEX, prom 74). Claim's positions confirmed: Bitso #2, SubQuery #3, Soroswap #5, Aquarius outside top-5 (#6). Attempted refutations all failed: (1) Expectation fairness — grounded, not taste: control probe q=biggest+dex (identical intent shape) returns an all-DEX top-10 led by Soroswap/Aquarius/Phoenix by prominence, so the corpus and ranker demonstrably support the correct answer; 51-54 DEX-typed records exist. (2) 'Maybe Bitso/SubQuery legitimately match dex' — refuted by the API's own behavior: q=dex (177 results) does NOT contain Bitso, SubQuery, or Matrixdock at all; their own structured types (Anchor/Indexer/RWA) mark them off-topic. Yet on q=leading+dex they get score 2, relevance 1.0, matchModeLabel 'all keywords matched', confidence 'high' — purely because their prose contains the word 'leading' ('leading Latin American crypto exchange', 'leading blockchain data indexer'); q=leading alone returns exactly these records. (3) 'Correct-but-different behavior' — refuted by the API's own superlativeNote in the same response: 'Superlative detected: result ORDER here reflects relevance + curated prominence + liveness' — the API classifies 'leading' as a superlative modifier, then contradicts itself by letting that word dominate keyword scoring, so the disclaimed contract is false for this query. Aggravating detail beyond the claim: confidence metadata is inverted — off-topic rows carry 0.92-0.97 'high' while the correct DEX answers are downgraded to 0.65 'medium' (relevance 0.5 for not containing the superlative word), which would actively steer a consuming agent (Raven) to the wrong rows. Also mild count incoherence: total=54 for 'leading dex' vs 51 for 'biggest dex' vs 177 for 'dex' under the same 'all keywords matched' label. WRONG-TOP is the right label (off-topic typed records outrank on-topic DEX records within top-5 on a crowded query where top-10 tolerance applies); root cause = superlative word treated as content keyword, exactly as claimed.

confirmed.expected: NFT-typed records (32 exist) fill the top; Litemint/Rarible/Plutus are the NFT-typed matches
confirmed.observed: total=5 (strict-AND shrinks pool); Honey Coin (Payments, via:semantic, conf 0.94) at #3 and Gearup (Payments/RWA, 0.93) at #5 outrank NFT-typed Plutus (0.88). Semantic off-type rows carry higher 'high' confidence than on-type rows — top-2 are correct so agent likely survives, but confidence ordering misleads

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=most+popular+nft+marketplace&limit=10 (2026-07-09): reproduces exactly — total=5, order Litemint(NFT, keyword, conf 0.75 "high"), Rarible(NFT, keyword, conf 0.70 "medium"), Honey Coin(Payments, semantic, conf 0.94 "high", relevance 0.96), Plutus(NFT, semantic, conf 0.88), Gearup(Payments/RWA, semantic, conf 0.93). RANKING part of the claim REFUTED: the only true NFT marketplaces in the pool are #1-#2 (Litemint's description: "NFT marketplace on the Stellar network"; Rarible: "NFT marketplace... create, buy, and sell"). Plutus is NOT a marketplace by its own prose ("conditional NFT ownership... rental, mortgage, and lending" infrastructure), so Honey Coin at #3 vs Plutus at #4 is ordering among semantic-backfill non-answers (score 0.702 vs 0.686) — within tolerance, on-topic records fill the top. The "NFT-typed fill the top" expectation is also unfair under strict-AND: plain q=nft+marketplace returns total=34 with all-keyword rows on top; the superlative tokens shrink the keyword pool to 2 and semantic backfills 3, with an honest superlativeNote in meta. CONFIDENCE part CONFIRMED and systematic: Honey Coin — a Nairobi fiat-to-stablecoin anchor with zero NFT/marketplace content in its own record — carries confidence.relevance=0.96 / score 0.94 "high", while exact-match Rarible gets 0.70 "medium". Mechanism is a cross-lane scale mismatch: semantic rows map cosine ~0.68-0.70 to relevance 0.93-0.96, keyword rows use token-overlap relevance (0.67, diluted by the non-matching "most popular" tokens — same Litemint scores conf 0.97 on plain q=nft+marketplace). Cross-checked on unrelated query q=best+decentralized+identity+solution&limit=8: semantic idOS conf 0.95 outranks keyword exact-match DID:STELLAR conf 0.92 despite DID:STELLAR relevance=1.0, and off-topic DeFindex (Lending yield vaults) gets relevance 0.90 "high" on an identity query. Every semantic backfill row observed lands 0.86-0.95 "high" regardless of topical fit — an agent using confidence.label/score for trust calibration is inverted-misled, exactly as the claim's own hedge stated ("top-2 correct so agent likely survives, but confidence ordering misleads").
verdict.correctedMode: DATA-WRONG (not WRONG-TOP): result ordering is correct — the defect is the served confidence structured field itself. Semantic-lane confidence.relevance is rescaled cosine on an incomparable scale, so off-topic semantic rows are labeled "high" (0.86-0.96) above exact-keyword on-topic rows ("medium" 0.70). File as semantic-confidence-inflation affecting the confidence contract, severity low-medium since rank order and the superlativeNote remain honest.

PROBE 2 re-run: /api/research?q=upgrade%20stellar%20sdk%20to%20latest%20version&limit=8 → rank 4 = "Software Versions | Stable v20.0.0 (December 18, 2023)" chunk, ranked ABOVE the Protocol 27 upgrade guide (stellar.org/blog .../protocol-27-upgrade-guide, rank 8) on a to-latest question. Same pattern.

FAIRNESS checks passed: rank 5 and rank 4 are within even the strict top-5 tolerance; the expectation derives from the doc's own data (its 2025 section exists in-corpus and ranks 1 elsewhere); not explained by correct-but-different behavior — the served content is factually obsolete (Futurenet deprecated, CLI v0.1.2 vs 23.x era), and both stale chunks carry confidence label "high".

ONE PRECISION REFINEMENT to the claim's freshness sub-point: freshness is not literally always 1.0 corpus-wide. Probe /api/research?q=protocol%2023%20upgrade%20guide&limit=10 shows dated sdf-blog items get freshness 0.6 (ageDays 395) while ALL dev-docs/cap chunks get freshness 1 with ageDays null. So the mechanism is worse than "blind": undated chunks default to maximum freshness, producing an inversion where the undated 2022 Preview-3 chunk scores freshness 1.0 while a dated 2025 Protocol-23 blog post scores 0.6. This is the ambiguous-null bug class (null publishedAt treated as maximally fresh) and is the root cause that lets the 2022 chunk win; it strengthens rather than weakens the finding.

verdict.evidence:
REPRODUCED. Re-ran the exact probe (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/research?q=stellar%20smart%20contract%20vulnerabilities%20common&limit=10): #1 and #2 are byte-identical CAP-0049/CAP-0048 'Simple Summary' one-liners — content is literally "## Simple Summary\n\nAllow smart contracts to interoperate with Stellar assets." — served at confidence 0.86 "high" (claim said 0.81; slightly different number, same behavior). ZERO audit/security content in top-10, exactly as claimed.

EXPECTATION IS FAIR — the corpus provably contains direct answers. (a) source=audit lane is rich: /api/research?q=security%20audit&source=audit returns CoinFabrik 'Methodology' chunks (BondHive/Excellar), OtterSec '05—GeneralFindings' (Soroban Governor, Blend), Veridise Soroban Core methodology, etc. (b) The unfiltered lane serves this content when embeddings match: q=reentrancy%20overflow%20arithmetic%20errors → audit chunks at #2-#4,#6,#8-#10 (Runtime Verification OverflowAndUnderflow, OtterSec Soroswap Integer Overflow, Halborn, Veridise underflow findings). (c) Crucially, a natural-language paraphrase of the SAME intent works: q=what%20are%20common%20vulnerabilities%20in%20stellar%20smart%20contracts → 10/10 audit docs incl. OtterSec Axelar '04—Vulnerabilities' at 0.88; q=common%20security%20vulnerabilities%20in%20soroban%20contracts → 8/10 audit. So this is not a corpus gap and not correct-but-different behavior — the keyword-style phrasing ("stellar smart contract vulnerabilities [common]") loses the vulnerability intent to 'smart contract' lexical mass and boilerplate one-liner chunks win, at HIGH-labeled confidence. Nearby phrasing "stellar smart contract vulnerabilities" also mostly fails (1/10 audit, plus a junk '2024-08-23' date-titled chunk at #8), so the probe is not a one-off embedding fluke.

Top-10 tolerance is generous and still 0/10 on-topic; the #1/#2 records' own content contains nothing about vulnerabilities. CONFIRMED WRONG-TOP, with two aggravating sub-signals worth carrying into the fix: (1) duplicate contentless 'Simple Summary'/'Implementation' boilerplate chunks are rankable and get relevance-floored 'high' confidence; (2) failure is phrasing-sensitive — keyword queries (typical of agent tool calls) fail where full NL questions succeed.

(1) https://stellarlight.xyz/api/research?q=slender&source=audit&limit=10 → rank #1 is the exact-match record: {"title":"Slender — certora (Slender Security Assessment Report)","protocol":"Slender","publishedAt":"2024-05-21","confidence":{"score":0.4,"label":"low","relevance":0.14,"freshness":1,"authority":0.9}}. verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED — reproduced exactly and survived every refutation attempt. Re-ran https://stellarlight.xyz/api/research?q=upcoming%20stellar%20events&limit=8 (2026-07-09T23:27Z): all 8 results are the same 'Upcoming Events' section family from weekly roundups, ranked: #1 'week of May 15' (pub 2026-05-22, conf 0.73, rel 0.72), #2 'week of May 22' (pub 2026-05-29, conf 0.73, rel 0.71) ... #8 'week of Jun 26' (pub 2026-07-03, conf 0.68, rel 0.63, ageDays 6, freshness 0.99). Refutation checks: (1) Fairness of 'expected' record — rank #8's content lists 'July 9: Stellar Developers Meeting', 'July 10: StellarX PH Demo Day Metro Manila', 'July 10: Stellar Community Meet Up, Istanbul' — genuinely upcoming as of 2026-07-09; the #1-2 May lists end June 5 (100% past); even rank #6 (pub Jun 26) ends '~July 4' — all past. So the Jul-3 roundup is indeed the ONLY list answering 'upcoming events'. (2) Fresher record missed? Two corpus probes ('stellar weekly roundup', 'week of July roundup town hall') surface no roundup newer than pub 2026-07-03 — it is the freshest in-corpus. (3) Correct-but-different behavior? No: query has explicit temporal intent, relevance is homogeneous (0.63–0.72 across identical-format sections; note relevance is anti-correlated with recency here, i.e. embedding noise), and the scoring math makes the failure structural: max freshness contribution spread = 0.15×(0.99−0.93) ≈ 0.009 vs relevance-noise spread 0.65×(0.72−0.63) ≈ 0.059 — the 0.15 freshness weight can never reorder this family. (4) Tolerance fair? Yes — omitting &limit=8 returns the identical 8-result list (8 is the default), so #1-2 all-past / current-answer-dead-last is the exact default response an agent consumes. STALE-SERVED is the correct label: outdated content served at top where current exists in-corpus.

CONFIRMED — reproduced 2026-07-09 against live API; every element of the claim held up, and the expectation is contract-grounded, not taste.

1) skill= aliases to naive substring q — proven three ways: (a) response meta echoes the param as q: `"filters":{"q":"rust","location":null,...}` with no skill key; (b) /api/builders?q=rust and ?skill=rust return byte-identical ordering [samogera, Malika7188, CaBsCrypto, pedro-pelicioni, G0vermind]; (c) the live OpenAPI spec (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/openapi.json) documents q as "Free-text filter over bio / role / projects (accepts `skill`/`tech` as aliases)" while separately documenting skill as "Filter by skill/tech mentioned in bio" — so skill is a canonical documented param whose contract ("skill/tech mentioned in bio") the implementation does not honor.

2) skill=rust (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/builders?skill=rust): 5 results exactly as claimed. #1 samogera matches only via bio substring "make tRUSTt possible" ('...make trust possible...'), #3 CaBsCrypto via project "Trust Leaf", #5 G0vermind via "rustic forestry operations" — 3/5 false positives. The two builders whose own bios state Rust ("experience in Go, Rust, and Python" — Malika7188; "strong background in Rust, Solidity" — pedro-pelicioni) sit at #2 and #4, both below the trust-match at #1. WRONG-TOP confirmed.

3) skill=go (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/builders?skill=go): 24 results. Match-context extraction shows the substring hits: "SantiaGO, Chile" (blessedux #1), "GOmes" (#2, #8, #22 walterkochergomez via username), "SantiaGO Villarreal" (#3), "ChatPay GO Labs" company name (#4, #17, #18), "ThiaGO/TiaGO" (#5, #6, #7), "HugGO" (#9), "cateGOry" (pedro #15), "GOnçalves/GOnzález" (#16, #12), "GOvernor/neuroGOvernance" (#21), "laGOs" (#13), "GOdstime" (#14), "anGOla" (#19), "saraGOssy" (#20), "amancio"→"rodriGO" (#11), "public GOods" (#24), "DieGO" (#23). The only builder whose own data states Go as a skill is Malika7188, at position 10 — behind 9 name/location false positives. 1/24 ≈ 4.2% precision, matching the claimed ~4%.

Fairness checks passed: skill is documented in the spec (not an invented param); the expected records' own bios literally state the skills; refutation angles (undocumented param, correct-but-different tokenization, alternate real Go devs among the 24) all failed — no other returned builder's data states Go/Rust skill. Mode label WRONG-TOP is correct (off-topic substring matches outrank the on-topic builders at #1); root cause is non-word-boundary substring matching plus skill→q aliasing that ignores the documented "mentioned in bio" scope.

verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED, and root cause identified. (1) Re-ran https://stellarlight.xyz/api/clusters — serves category clusters: Anchor {"size":1,"scfFundedCount":0,"crowdedness":1}, Asset size=3, User-Facing App size=202, Protocol/Contract size=88; category sizes sum to exactly 500. ?dimension=types serves Payments size=124, Bridge size=37.
