(2) Ground truth from the SAME corpus: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/analyze?dimension=categories (self-labeled scope "Active projects only... 888") serves Anchor=19 (scfFundedCount=5), Asset=32, User-Facing App=349, Protocol/Contract=130; category browse /api/projects/search?category=Anchor confirms counts.total=19 (Asset 32, UFA 350, P/C 133). Clusters meta has NO scope note — only the crowdedness-formula note, which tells agents to "cross-reference .size ... for the raw numbers", presenting the deflated size as authoritative. (3) Root cause (read-only source check, src/app/api/clusters/route.ts ~line 84-90): the route queries payload.find({collection:"projects", where:{status:{in:["Development","Pre-Release","Live"]}}, limit: 500}) — the IDENTICAL active-scope analyze counts as 888, but hard-capped at 500 docs, so all bucket sizes and crowdedness scores are computed from an arbitrary first-500 truncation (hence the sum=500 smoking gun). This refutes the only correct-but-different-behavior defense: it is not an intentional repo-linked scope, it is truncation against the endpoint's own intended scope. (4) Impact verified: with true inputs Anchor crowdedness = round(log2(19+1)+log2(5+1)) ≈ 7, but the endpoint serves 1 — a wrong "underbuilt lane" market-gap read exactly as claimed. (5) Corrections that don't weaken the finding: the claim's expected type-level figures (Payments=246, Bridge=45) are not verifiable via the live API (analyze rejects dimension=types; projects/search silently ignores a types= param, returning total 0), and the claim's "likely repo-linked-only scope" mechanism guess is wrong — actual mechanism is the limit:500 query cap. DATA-WRONG label holds (served structured fields size/crowdedness are false vs the corpus's own counts).

Refutation attempts all failed: (1) not a fluke — q=xkcdblorptok gives KES/COCA Wallet/QCAD at 0.91–0.92 "high", relevance 1.0, same false label; (2) confidence does not discriminate noise from signal — genuine query q=decentralized+exchange+amm returns Soroswap/Switchly/Lumenswap via=keyword raw score 3.0 at confidence 0.97 vs 0.92 for pure noise at raw 0.65 (semantic floor), both labeled "high" with relevance=1.0, confirming rank-relative normalization inflates noise; (3) realistic scenario confirmed — q=safemoon (real crypto, not on Stellar) returns Stellar Dashboard/SoroStarter/Walletban at 0.92 "high" relevance 1.0, so "is X on Stellar?" agents are confidently misled; (4) the honest signals that do exist (meta.semantic:true, via:"semantic") are contradicted by the purpose-built confidence object AND the affirmatively-false matchModeLabel. Two independent defects: (a) rank-relative relevance normalization makes the top-of-noise always relevance≈1.0 → confidence "high"; (b) matchModeLabel:"all keywords matched" is served on semantic-fallback responses where no keyword matched. Fix direction: gate confidence on absolute semantic score (0.65≈floor), label semantic fallback honestly (e.g. "no keyword match — nearest semantic neighbors"), and/or mirror the repos-lane honest-0 behavior below a similarity threshold.

verdict.evidence:
CONFIRMED, with one framing correction. Re-ran all probes 2026-07-09:

1. Probe reproduces: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=reflector&limit=3 → Reflector (#1, score 1) has "types":[] and "supportedNetworks":[], while its own shortDescription reads "Reflector is a decentralized price oracle and data-feed network for Stellar and Soroban smart contracts." Structured field empty vs record's own prose = DATA-WRONG.

2. Systematic, not a one-record miss: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=price+oracle&limit=5 → top-5 = reflector, dia, band, lightecho, redstone-finance — ALL five have types=[]. The entire oracle class is untagged. (Retrieval itself is fine, as the claim said.)

3. "No Oracle among the 21 types" verified exactly: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/clusters?dimension=types returns 21 clusters (Bridge 37, RWA 57, Security 65, Payments 124, SDK 87, DEX 28, Analytics 29, AI 26, Wallet 26, NFT 17, Education 13, Anchor 12, Gaming 11, Lending 10, Social Impact 11, RPC 9, Indexer 3, Explorer 3, Faucet 2, Infrastructure 1, Stablecoin 1) — no Oracle. Niche tags with 1-3 members exist, so "too small to tag" is not the design.

4. Type-lane consumer impact is concrete: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/clusters?dimension=types&key=Oracle → counts.returned=0 with advisory "No cluster matches 'Oracle'", while keyword q=oracle finds 24 projects. Control key=Lending returns 1 cluster.

5. Credio angle confirmed: untangled's prose contains "...and Credio, a risk or[acle]..." but types=['RWA','Lending'] — secondary oracle capability unrepresented.

6. Framing correction (strengthens the finding): the claim says the taxonomy "should be able to express Oracle" as if it structurally cannot. In fact the live OpenAPI spec (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/openapi.json) ALREADY advertises Oracle: the Project.types description reads "Capability tags (Wallet, DEX, Lending, Oracle, SDK, RPC, Faucet, NFT, RWA, Anchor, Stablecoin, Indexer, ...)" and the /api/clusters description reads "dimension=types (finer: Wallet/DEX/Lending/RWA/Oracle…)". So Oracle is a documented tag with ZERO usage in data — this is DATA-WRONG plus live-API⇄OpenAPI drift (a spec-following consumer querying the advertised Oracle tag gets a legitimate-looking 0).

Fairness check passed: expectation derives from the record's own prose + the spec's own documented tag list, not opinion. No correct-but-different explanation exists — Reflector carries no alternative structured signal (category=Infrastructure is the coarse 7-value enum, no oracle value there either).
verdict.correctedMode: DATA-WRONG (label correct; note it is also spec-drift — "Oracle" is a documented types value in the live OpenAPI spec with zero data usage, not an inexpressible concept)
